theme_name: The Illusion of the Material World

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Know then that Nature is Maya, and that the great God is the Lord of Maya.

critical_distinctions: UPN treats materiality as fundamentally illusory (Maya); Abrahamic texts treat it as real but transient and corruptible.

counter_evidence.text: Torah affirms the material creation as 'very good' (Gen 1:31).

theme_name: The Principle of Correspondence (As Above, So Below)

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: As above, so below, as below, so above.

critical_distinctions: ETT/ZOH view this as mechanical occult law; NTB views this as a petition for divine alignment.

counter_evidence.text: QUR emphasizes strict separation and transcendence of the Creator over creation (Tanzih).

critical_distinctions: UPN/BG: Atman is literally identical to ultimate reality (Brahman); TOR/ZOH: divine breath is a gift that remains distinct from God; NTB: indwelling kingdom, not identity; QUR: the Ruh is breathed in but God remains wholly transcendent (no ontological union); ETT: immortal spark as cosmological substance; DDJ/ICH: Te as innate virtue aligned with Tao, not a personal soul.

counter_evidence.text: QUR strictly prohibits hulul (divine indwelling/incarnation), making the Ruh a delegated property, not a divine identity (Surah 42:11 — nothing is like unto Him).

counter_evidence.text: NTB's exclusive claim ('no man cometh... but by me') structurally conflicts with all pluralist path frameworks (DDJ, BG, UPN), making this a convergence of metaphor rather than doctrine.

counter_evidence.text: Mainstream Sunni Islam rejects Fana as a valid doctrinal concept; the cited verse (2:156) does not itself teach ego annihilation but rather patient acceptance of loss.

counter_evidence.text: Abrahamic traditions unanimously reject reincarnation as heresy. Hebrews 9:27 states 'it is appointed for men to die once' — a direct structural contradiction of the karma/gilgul framework.

critical_distinctions: ICH/DDJ: numbers as cosmological generative sequences — not objects of worship; ZOH: Sefirot as divine emanation architecture — number as ontological structure; TOR/NTB/QUR: sacred number as divine signature in creation and prophecy; BG/UPN: cosmic timescales as framework for liberation cycles; ETT: numerical planes as literal metaphysical tiers. counter_evidence.text: QUR explicitly warns against divination and numerology (Surah 5:90), creating tension between structural seven-heaven cosmology and any system that treats number as a divinatory or esoteric key.

counter_evidence.text: Some traditions, such as certain forms of Buddhism, do not emphasize fasting or purification as a means of spiritual growth.

Then those who feared the Lord spoke with one another. The Lord paid attention and heard them, and a book of remembrance was written before him of those who feared the Lord and esteemed his name.
The Book of the Generations of Adam, the book of the generations of the world.

critical_distinctions: While the concept of the Collective Unconscious/Akashic is present in various traditions, the nature and scope of this concept differ significantly. For example, the Akashic Records in the Emerald Tablets are seen as a repository of individual soul records, whereas the Collective Qi in the Dao De Jing is a more general concept of a universal energy field.

counter_evidence.text: Some scholars argue that the concept of the Collective Unconscious/Akashic is not present in all traditions, and that the similarities between the concepts are due to cultural exchange and syncretism.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: As the rivers flowing east and west merge in the sea and become one with it, forgetting they were ever separate, so do all creatures lose their separateness when they merge at last in Brahman.
Whatever exists in the universe is the manifestation of the Supreme Being, and the Supreme Being is the ultimate reality.
The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao; the name that can be named is not the eternal name.
Say, He is Allah, the One and Only; Allah, the Eternal, Absolute; He begets not, nor is He begotten; And there is none like unto Him.
That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.
Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God is one Lord.
The Ein Sof is the infinite, the boundless, the endless, the unity of all.
As above, so below; as within, so without; the universe is one, and all is one.
The Creative is the beginning of all things, and the Receptive is the origin of all things.

critical_distinctions: While the concept of unity is present in all traditions, the nature and characteristics of this unity vary. For example, the Advaita Vedanta tradition sees the ultimate reality as a unified, all-encompassing consciousness, while the Quranic concept of Tawhid emphasizes the oneness of God as a singular, indivisible entity.

counter_evidence.text: The concept of unity is not universally accepted, and some traditions emphasize the diversity and multiplicity of existence.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The superior man sets his person at rest before he moves; he composes his mind before he speaks; he makes his relations firm before he asks for something.
And it shall be our righteousness, if we observe to do all this commandment before the Lord our God, as he hath commanded us.
Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.

Righteousness is not that you turn your faces towards the east or the west, but [true] righteousness is [in] one who believes in Allah, the Last Day, the angels, the Book, and the prophets and gives wealth, in spite of love for it, to relatives, orphans, the needy, the traveler, those who ask [for help], and for freeing slaves; [and who] establishes prayer and gives zakah; [those who] fulfill their promise when they promise; and [those who] are patient in poverty and hardship and during battle.
The Three that give the Four; the Four that give the Seven; the Seven that give the Ten; the Ten that give the Three that give the Four.
Better is one's own dharma, though imperfect, than the dharma of another well performed.
The righteous are the foundation of the world, and the world is sustained by them.
A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving.
The Self is the source of all dharma.

critical_distinctions: While the concept of Right Action (Dharma) is present across various traditions, the specific interpretations and emphasis differ. For example, in Hinduism and Buddhism, dharma is often associated with one's duty and moral order, whereas in Christianity and Islam, righteousness is emphasized as a key aspect of faith.

counter_evidence.text: Some scholars argue that the concept of dharma is not universally applicable and may be culturally specific.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a path to spiritual growth
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of me. Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a test of faith
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient, who, when disaster strikes them, say, 'Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return.' Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy. And it is those who are the guided.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a result of karma
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The one who has controlled the mind and senses, and has realized the Self, is eligible to attain the state of supreme peace and liberation. The yogi is greater than the ascetic, greater than the empiricist and greater than the fruitive worker. Therefore, O Arjuna, in all circumstances, be a yogi.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a natural part of life
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom. Mastering others is strength; mastering yourself is true power.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a result of ignorance
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: As the rivers, flowing east and west, merge in the sea and become one with it, forgetting they were ever separate, so do all creatures lose their separateness when they merge at last in Brahman.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a punishment for sin
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: However, if you do not obey the Lord your God and do not carefully follow all his commands and decrees I am giving you today, all these curses will come on you and overtake you: You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the country. tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a means of spiritual purification
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Rabbi Simeon said: 'The sufferings of the righteous are more precious than all the commandments and good deeds, for through them the Holy One, blessed be He, is glorified in the world.'

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a result of imbalance
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: The Seven Lords of Amenti, they who guard the gates of the universe, they who are the keepers of the balance, they who are the bringers of the cycles, they who are the rulers of the stars.

tradition_connections.concept: Suffering as a natural part of the cycle of life
tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: Heng. Persevering. The firm and the yielding unite in harmony. The yielding is the foundation of the firm. The firm is the completion of the yielding.

critical_distinctions: While the concept of suffering is present in all the traditions, the understanding and interpretation of its nature and purpose vary significantly. Some traditions view suffering as a punishment for sin, while others see it as a means of spiritual purification or a natural part of the cycle of life.

counter_evidence.text: The concept of suffering is not universally accepted as a means of spiritual growth or purification. Some traditions view suffering as an inherent part of the human condition, rather than a means to an end.
counter_evidence.source: The Buddha's teachings on suffering, as recorded in the Pali Canon, emphasize the importance of understanding and overcoming suffering, rather than viewing it as a means of spiritual growth.

tradition_connections.verbatim_quote: As all things are from One, by the thought of One, so all things are born from this one thing by adaptation.
Knowing the masculine, but keeping to the feminine, one becomes a ravine, and the ravine's virtue will never be exhausted.

critical_distinctions: The concept of the alchemical marriage varies across traditions, but often represents the union of opposites, such as masculine and feminine, or spiritual and material.

counter_evidence.text: Some traditions, such as certain interpretations of the Quran, do not explicitly mention the concept of the alchemical marriage.

theme_name: Symbolism of the Tree of Life

Those who believe and do righteous deeds - the Most Merciful will appoint for them love.

critical_distinctions: The symbolism of the Tree of Life varies across traditions, but often represents a connection between the divine and human realms, and the pursuit of knowledge and wisdom.

counter_evidence.text: Some scholars argue that the concept of sacred numbers and numerology is not a universal theme across traditions, but rather a product of cultural and historical context.

theme_name: The Role of Ritual and Ceremony

counter_evidence.text: Some scholars argue that rituals and ceremonies are not essential to spiritual growth and that they can even be a hindrance to true understanding.

critical_distinctions: While the concept of the spiritual warrior appears across various traditions, there are distinct differences in the way it is understood and applied.
